title: Supercharge Your AI Coding Workflow: Meet abtop, Your Ultimate Agent Monitor!
article: Ever found yourself juggling multiple AI coding assistants like Claude Code, Codex CLI, or OpenCode across different projects? It’s a fantastic way to boost productivity, but let's be honest, keeping track of all those sessions, their token usage, and potential rate limits can quickly become a headache. You might be wondering if you're about to hit an API quota, or perhaps an agent spun up a temporary server and forgot to shut it down. This scattered experience can slow you down and lead to unnecessary frustration. Imagine having a single, elegant dashboard right in your terminal that gives you a crystal-clear overview of all your active AI coding agent sessions. That's exactly what `abtop` brings to the table. Think of it as the `btop` for your AI agents – a beautiful, real-time system monitor tailored specifically for your intelligent coding companions. It shows you everything from current token usage and how full your context window is, to impending rate limits, any child processes spawned, and even open network ports. All this information is presented at a glance, allowing you to stay on top of your AI-powered workflow effortlessly. What makes `abtop` particularly clever is how it works. It intelligently discovers your active Claude Code, Codex CLI, and OpenCode sessions by analyzing local process and file states. This means it doesn't need any sensitive API keys or complex authentication – it's entirely read-only, focusing solely on providing you with crucial operational insights. Whether you're on macOS, Linux, or Windows, `abtop` integrates seamlessly, offering native support across all major platforms with straightforward installation options via `curl`, `cargo`, or `PowerShell`. For developers, the benefits are immediate and tangible. If you're running several agents, `abtop` consolidates their status into one screen, eliminating the need to jump between multiple terminals or logs. Hitting rate limits becomes a thing of the past, as you can watch your quota in real-time and adjust your usage proactively. No more worrying about orphaned processes or forgotten ports – `abtop` will flag them, helping you maintain a clean development environment. Plus, with per-session context window percentage bars, you'll know exactly when your agent is nearing its information capacity, allowing you to refine your prompts or switch strategies before hitting a wall. And for `tmux` users, there's an incredible feature: press Enter on an agent in `abtop` to instantly jump to its corresponding `tmux` pane! It even comes with a variety of themes to match your terminal aesthetic. `abtop` empowers you to manage your AI coding agents with unprecedented clarity and control. It transforms a potentially chaotic multi-agent setup into a streamlined, efficient, and enjoyable experience. It's not just a monitoring tool; it's a productivity enhancer that helps you get the most out of your AI assistants, ensuring you're always informed and never caught off guard.
key_takeaways: Provides a unified, real-time dashboard for multiple AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex CLI, OpenCode).
Monitors critical metrics like token usage, context window percentage, and API rate limits to prevent interruptions.
Helps maintain a clean development environment by detecting orphaned processes and open ports.
Offers seamless cross-platform support and integrates with `tmux` for enhanced workflow management.
Read-only operation means no API keys or complex authentication are required, ensuring security and ease of use.
readme_summary: abtop is a terminal-based monitoring tool for AI coding agents, inspired by htop. It provides real-time insights into the performance and resource usage of agents like Claude Code, Codex CLI, and OpenCode, displaying token consumption, rate limits, context window status, and active network ports.
description: Like htop, but for AI coding agents. Monitor Claude    Code & Codex CLI sessions, tokens, context window,    rate limits, and ports in real-time.
